title: solena scandens
intro: Solena scandens, also known as the wild passionflower or creeping passionflower, is a tropical vine native to Central and South America, characterized by its twining stems and intricate flowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antiviral properties. The health benefits of Solena scandens include reducing anxiety and stress, improving sleep quality, and alleviating symptoms of menopause and menstrual cramps. Solena scandens can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, with the flowers and leaves typically being used in equal proportions to make the herbal preparation.
